page: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-16-highlssd is a memory-optimized and storage-intensive server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a 105 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 128 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is bundled with 6442 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity, while no GPUs are present.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for most compute and memory operations, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1691.0 and a multi-core score of 10293.0. The Intel Xeon processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which assist in specialized workloads. In LLM inference tests, the instance achieves a strong text generation speed of 264.49 tokens per second on a small 135M model, though performance drops to an average of 12.73 tokens per second for a medium 7B model. Memory bandwidth tests show 1088.32 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 91.92 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the z3-highmem-16-highlssd offers a high-density resource profile by combining substantial memory capacity with a massive 6.4 TB local NVMe SSD footprint. This dense hardware configuration is highly suitable for memory-bound and storage-intensive workloads, such as database operations, caching layers, and data processing. However, the lack of GPU acceleration makes it unsuitable for heavy deep learning training, and its CPU performance remains within average bounds for general-purpose compute tasks. It is best utilized for relational databases, Redis deployments, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-16-highlssd is a memory-optimized and storage-intensive server instance designed for demanding data workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C x86_64 processor running at 2.7 GHz, it provides 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores and a large 105 MB L3 cache. The instance is configured with 128 GB of RAM and a substantial 6442 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, with no GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate average CPU and memory performance, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 10293.0 and a strong text generation speed of 264.49 tokens per second on small LLM models. With its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and massive local storage, this instance is highly cost-efficient for memory-heavy and I/O-intensive applications. It is ideally suited for database operations, caching layers, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-16-highlssd is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 6442 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-16-highlssd features 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 6442 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized server combining dedicated Intel Xeon processing with massive local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor
128 GB of system memory delivering 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
6442 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector math
Well-suited for relational databases, caching layers, and web serving
